free_text: The study of nuclear fission induced by high-energy
protons
In Russian.
In Russian.
Earlier work of the
same group. Data for uranium ternary fission
cross section.
590 MeV
2.9 GeV
Natural muscovite mica detectors, each about
5-10 cm2 * 0.01 cm, formed a sandwich, with an
aluminum foil, 200 ug/cm2, in between, that was
covered on one side by target material.
Correlated
fission fragment tracks were observed after the
chemical treatment of the detectors.
The layers of natural uranium of about 5 ug/cm2
were obtained by electrospraying or by thermal
evaporation of uranium tetrafluoride.
The layers of bismuth, natural lead, gold, natural
tellurium, natural tin and natural silver of about
20-100 ug/cm2 were obtained by thermal evaporation
of metals.
Comment by compiler: no details are given on the
U-235 samples.
Performed: Loss of events as a result of their
passing through the aluminum foil; admixture of
false events due to interactions of incident protons
with heavy constituents of mica or heavy impurities
in the aluminum foil; accidental coincidences of
two single tracks; fission of heavy impurities in
the targets; non-uniformity of the target material.
Negligible: sensitivity of the monitor and fissile
layers to secondary particles.
Total number of observed binary tracks.
Distributions of projected track lengths
and projected angles between two tracks.
-By authors- For the determinations of cross section
for binary fission, the following scanning criteria
were adopted for correlated pairs of tracks:
- the track ranges exceeded 3 um;
- the distance between the fragment entry points
were less than about 20 um.
By Khlopin's Institute - A.P.
Status, Misc-Col and Data Sections
Are Corrected by a.P.
A.P.: Monitor corrected, REL-REF and
ternary fission data are added.
A.P.: Monitor and REL-REF are corrected.

to VML.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. MONIT
reactions updated.
Systematic errors resulting from the accepted values
of monitoring reaction cross sections are not
included in the quoted uncertainties.
The uncertainties in the target thickness,
the counting efficiency for the beam monitoring and
the correction factors. Statistics of the fission
events and the beam monitoring.
